relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Aaron and his sons are brought near for priestly service.
Holy garments are made for glory and beauty.
Wise hearts filled with spirit of wisdom craft the garments.
Breastpiece, ephod, robe, tunic, turban, and sash prepare holy service.
Gold and sacred colors become priestly material.
The ephod is designed in beauty and skill.
Shoulder pieces join it together.
The woven band belongs to the same work.
Two onyx stones carry Israel's names.
Six names on one stone, six on the other.
Names are engraved like signets and set in gold.
Aaron bears Israel on his shoulders before YHWH.
Gold settings are made.
Gold cords join the settings.
The breastpiece of judgment is crafted like the ephod.
It is square and doubled.
The first row of stones is set.
The second row shines.
The third row is placed.
The fourth row completes the twelve.
Each stone carries a tribe-name.
Gold chains are made for the breastpiece.
Gold rings are set on its ends.
Gold cords pass through the rings.
The cords connect to shoulder settings.
More rings secure the inner edge.
Rings on the ephod receive the breastpiece.
A blue cord binds breastpiece and ephod together.
Aaron bears Israel's names upon his heart.
Urim and Thummim rest over the heart of judgment.
The robe of the ephod is all blue.
Its opening is bound so it will not tear.
Pomegranates and bells ring around the hem.
Bell and pomegranate alternate in sacred rhythm.
The sound accompanies Aaron's entering and going out.
A gold plate declares Holy to YHWH.
It rests on the turban with blue cord.
Aaron bears holy-things burden before YHWH.
Linen tunic, turban, and sash complete the garments.
Aaron's sons receive garments of glory and beauty.
They are clothed, anointed, filled-hand, and sanctified.
Linen undergarments protect bodily dignity.
The garments guard priestly service before the holy.

contemplative_note: Exodus 28 clothes priesthood as remembrance: Israel on shoulders, Israel on heart, holiness on forehead, sound at the hem, and beauty in service rather than domination.
